what_it_does: It documents a reusable structural-layer template for per-file ledger entries and the deterministic MAP engines that build a neutral, disk-comparable inventory over a codebase, plus guidance on layering a typed interpretive verification-state on top without altering neutral facts.
observations: Defines a per-file unit spec with YAML frontmatter and a neutral facts-only body.
References three overlay/MAP engine scripts: _FORMAT.md, _build-index.mjs, and _circuit-scan.mjs.
Maps the structural implementation field to an interpretive verification-state enum (stub/patchwork, built-unverified, verified-by-use, suspect, design-only).
Explicitly forbids ranking, canon/legacy/best/should language in the neutral body.
Defines coverage as a mechanical set-diff between disk files and mapped entries.
Includes reuse caveats about overlay-specific synonym tables and hardcoded engine paths.
standouts: The strict separation between structural neutral facts and a typed interpretive verification-state.
Coverage proof via set-diff rather than trust.
The idea of repurposing overlay/MAP engines as a generic template.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, links, and history.
Uses lowercase/hyphenated identifiers and neutral terminology.
References external files by both absolute and relative paths.
concerns: Terms like 4B, :8000, :8007, and recollection's distill are used without explanation in this file.
The absolute Windows-style path /mnt/c/... may be environment-specific.
The claim of 100% coverage on vi-visual-dev/overlay is not substantiated within this file.
notes: A guide that defines a neutral per-file structural ledger format and the deterministic engines that index and verify it, with instructions for adding a typed interpretive verification layer on top.
questions: What do 4B, :8000, and :8007 refer to specifically?
What is the exact schema for the verification-state enum and which process applies it?
How is the set-diff coverage proof run and where is it recorded?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide a structural-layer template and composition guidance; the file does exactly that by specifying formats, engines, and reuse rules, so they align.
apparent_themes: Neutral, deterministic structural mapping before interpretation.
Reusability through templated engines rather than copy-paste.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a reusable, project-agnostic pattern for generating and indexing per-file code/design ledger entries, while keeping interpretive judgments explicitly layered and typed.
novelty: The explicit two-layer model where a neutral map-decides-nothing structural inventory is mechanically provable via set-diff, and a separate typed verification-state is overlaid by interpretive processes.
contribution: It contributes a concrete per-file ledger unit spec, a deterministic engine pattern for consolidation and circuit scanning, and a rule set for keeping structural facts neutral while layering typed verification states.
summary_for_embedding: A living guide that specifies a neutral per-file structural ledger template and deterministic overlay/MAP indexing engines, and explains how to layer a typed interpretive verification-state on top without introducing ranking or verdicts into the structural body.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to document and templatize a reusable structural-layer inventory system with mechanical coverage proof and a clean separation from interpretive verification.
